title: Alsine campestris
intro: Alsine campestris, also known as Alsine or Field Sandwort, is a low-growing, perennial plant in the Caryophyllaceae family, commonly found in dry, sandy areas and grasslands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health concerns. The bioactive constituents of Alsine campestris include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Alsine campestris include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Alsine campestris is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Alsine, which is a group of small, annual or biennial plants. Alsine campestris is further classified as a member of the tribe Caryophylleae, within the subfamily Caryophylloideae. The plant is native to Europe and parts of Asia, and is often found growing in moist, grassy areas. Alsine campestris is also known as Field Mouse-ear or Field Mouse-ear Chickweed.
uses: Alsine campestris, also known as field speedwell, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to make infusions and teas that can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Alsine campestris is also thought to have antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and promote overall health. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.
benefits: Alsine campestris, also known as field mouse-ear cress, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues.
